rows.criterion: committed pre-crash files survive restart, cross-module consistent (btree->catalog->content)
rows.covered_by: loop asserting Lookup/Get/Read for all 3 committed fileState entries incl. the appended one

rows.criterion: committed Append's content is durable and correct post-recovery
rows.covered_by: committedFS.content includes appended suffix; cs2.Read compared byte-for-byte

rows.criterion: torn/incomplete mutation does not appear anywhere post-recovery (no partial/corrupted file visible)
rows.covered_by: btree.Lookup not-found, catalog.Get ErrNotFound, PrefixScan count excludes it, os.Stat(ContentPath) IsNotExist
catalog.RecoverFromWAL call in gen2 setup + assertions depend on its result

rows.criterion: btree state reconstructed consistently across restart
rows.covered_by: SaveRoot pre-crash / LoadRoot post-restart + NodeStore's own durable WriteNode persistence; documented finding: no WAL-based btree rebuild needed/used

rows.criterion: recovered system remains usable, not just readable
rows.covered_by: post-recovery Create+insertPath of a brand-new file via gen2 handles, read back
single-threaded test body; run under go test -race
